mechanism_narrative: Inflation running above a critical threshold while the federal funds rate remains near zero signals an imminent, aggressive Fed tightening cycle. Markets price forward discount-rate increases ahead of actual hikes, compressing equity valuations and producing negative SPX returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 281.933 with YoY inflation well above Fed 2% target as of 2022-02
regime_hint: high inflation regime preceding monetary tightening
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or statistically indistinguishable from unconditional mean in more than 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 7%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payroll employment signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue prospects, which is incorporated into equity prices with a lag, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 149629 thousand reflecting continued labor market recovery as of 2022-01
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month growth shows no significant positive correlation with subsequent 20-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales signal robust consumer spending momentum that feeds directly into near-term corporate earnings expectations, producing a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 prices within the following trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 626833 million dollars as of 2021-12 showing consumer spending strength
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following positive RSAFS month-over-month prints are not significantly different from returns following flat/negative prints (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity utilization and corporate output growth, which supports earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.8926 as of 2021-12 indicating continued industrial expansion
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC between INDPRO trailing 3-month growth and 60-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.03, or negative INDPRO growth periods show equal or higher forward returns than positive growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a level consistent with full employment, labor market tightness raises wage-price spiral fears and expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, causing equity markets to price in a higher discount rate and negative forward returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.0% as of 2022-01, approaching pre-pandemic lows
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following UNRATE readings below 3.8% are positive or indistinguishable from baseline in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation substantially exceeds the federal funds rate, real interest rates turn deeply negative, indicating the Fed is behind the curve. This joint condition signals rising stagflation risk and future rapid tightening, which together depress equity valuations over a quarterly horizon, an effect not captured by either series alone.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY inflation minus FEDFUNDS exceeds 5 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% while CPI inflation running far above target as of 2022-02
regime_hint: negative real interest rate / stagflation risk regime
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns in periods with real-rate gap exceeding 5pp are not significantly more negative than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts occurring simultaneously with an environment of near-term expected federal funds rate increases signals that rate-sensitive housing demand is beginning to contract ahead of broader economic softening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month. Neither falling housing starts nor low current rates alone is informative; the combination of contraction plus tightening expectation is required.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS is on a rising trajectory
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1702 thousand units as of 2021-12 amid anticipated Fed liftoff
regime_hint: early monetary tightening cycle with softening housing demand
failure_condition: Instances meeting the joint HOUST decline and FEDFUNDS rise condition show SPX 20-day forward returns that are positive or statistically indistinguishable from baseline (p > 0.05) in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a deceleration of real GDP growth below a critical annualized rate signals the economy is approaching a growth inflection point, leading forward-looking equity markets to price in reduced earnings growth and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 2%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19805.962 billion chained dollars as of 2021-Q3/Q4 reflecting decelerating post-recovery growth
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following GDPC1 growth prints below 2% annualized are not significantly more negative than returns following higher growth prints (t-test p > 0.05) across at least two economic cycles.
